revision.text:
'''Yakusanoikazuchi''' or '''ikazuchi''' is a collective name for the eight [[kami]] of [[thunder]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. Each one represents a different type of storm.<ref>{{Cite book|last1=Fr?d?ric|first1=Louis|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&q=Ikazuchi+myth&pg=PA376|title=Japan Encyclopedia|last2=Louis-Frédéric|date=2005|publisher=Harvard University Press|isbn=978-0-674-01753-5|pages=376|language=en}}</ref>
Ikazuchi (雷) literally means thunder in Japanese.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.japandict.com/%E9%9B%B7|title=JapanDict Japanese dictionary - Definition of 雷|website=www.japandict.com}}</ref>
== Mythology == Some tellings say that the eight deities were from the maggots of [[Izanami|Izanami's]] rotting corpse. Many versions of the [[Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki]] state that after [[Izanagi]] saw his wife in such a state and fled in terror, Izanami sent the [[Shikome|Yomotsu-shikome]], 150 warriors, and the eight thunder kami after him.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=175|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yakusanoikazuchi|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|last=Williams|first=George|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=-hmFOZe7m4MC&dq=eight+thunder+kami&pg=PA49|title=Shinto|date=2009|publisher=Infobase Publishing|isbn=978-1-4381-0646-5|pages=49|language=en}}</ref>
== List ==
* '''Fusu-ikazuchi:''' (Couchant thunder)<ref>{{Cite book|last=Herbert|first=Jean|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&dq=ikazuchi+shinto&pg=PT347|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|date=2010-10-18|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=May 2024}} * '''Hono-ikazuchi:''' (Fire thunder) the Kojiki says that this kami came from Izanami's breasts. * '''Kuro-ikazuchi:''' (Black thunder) The Kojiki says that this deity came from Izanami's belly. However, the Nihon Shoki says that Kuro-ikazuchi was in Izanami's [[rectum]]. * '''Naru-ikazuchi:''' (Rumbling thunder) * '''Ō-ikazuchi:''' (Great thunder) both the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] state that Ō-ikazuchi came from Izanami's head. * '''Saku-ikazuchi:''' (Cleaving thunder) * '''Tsuchi-ikazuchi:''' (Earth thunder) * '''Waka-ikazuchi:''' (Young thunder)
== References == {{reflist}}
[[Category:Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Thunder gods]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]]
{{Japan-myth-stub}}

* '''Fusu-ikazuchi:''' (Couchant thunder)<ref>{{Cite book|last=Herbert|first=Jean|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&dq=ikazuchi+shinto&pg=PT347|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|date=2010-10-18|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=May 2024}} * '''Hono-ikazuchi:''' (Fire thunder) the Kojiki says that this kami came from Izanami's breasts. * '''Kuro-ikazuchi:''' (Black thunder) The Kojiki says that this deity came from Izanami's belly. However, the Nihon Shoki says that Kuro-ikazuchi was in Izanami's [[rectum]]. * '''Naru-ikazuchi:''' (Rumbling thunder) * '''Ō-ikazuchi:''' (Great thunder) both the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] state that Ō-ikazuchi came from Izanami's head. * '''Saku-ikazuchi:''' (Cleaving thunder) * '''Tsuchi-ikazuchi:''' (Earth thunder) * '''Waka-ikazuchi:''' (Young thunder)

Many versions of the [[Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki]] state that after [[Izanagi]] saw his wife in such a state and fled in terror, Izanami sent the [[Shikome|Yomotsu-shikome]], 150 warriors, and the eight thunder kami after him.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=175|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yakusanoikazuchi|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|last=Williams|first=George|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=-hmFOZe7m4MC&dq=eight+thunder+kami&pg=PA49|title=Shinto|date=2009|publisher=Infobase Publishing|isbn=978-1-4381-0646-5|pages=49|language=en}}</ref>
== List ==
* '''Fusu-ikazuchi:''' (Couchant thunder)<ref>{{Cite book|last=Herbert|first=Jean|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&dq=ikazuchi+shinto&pg=PT347|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|date=2010-10-18|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=May 2024}} * '''Hono-ikazuchi:''' (Fire thunder) the Kojiki says that this kami came from Izanami's breasts. * '''Kuro-ikazuchi:''' (Black thunder) The Kojiki says that this deity came from Izanami's belly. However, the Nihon Shoki says that Kuro-ikazuchi was in Izanami's [[rectum]]. * '''Naru-ikazuchi:''' (Rumbling thunder) * '''Ō-ikazuchi:''' (Great thunder) both the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] state that Ō-ikazuchi came from Izanami's head. * '''Saku-ikazuchi:''' (Cleaving thunder) * '''Tsuchi-ikazuchi:''' (Earth thunder) * '''Waka-ikazuchi:''' (Young thunder)

* '''Fusu-ikazuchi:''' (Couchant thunder)<ref>{{Cite book|last=Herbert|first=Jean|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&dq=ikazuchi+shinto&pg=PT347|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|date=2010-10-18|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=May 2024}} * '''Hono-ikazuchi:''' (Fire thunder) the Kojiki says that this kami came from Izanami's breasts. * '''Kuro-ikazuchi:''' (Black thunder) The Kojiki says that this deity came from Izanami's belly. However, the Nihon Shoki says that Kuro-ikazuchi was in Izanami's [[rectum]]. * '''Naru-ikazuchi:''' (Rumbling thunder) * '''Ō-ikazuchi:''' (Great thunder) both the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] state that Ō-ikazuchi came from Izanami's head. * '''Saku-ikazuchi:''' (Cleaving thunder) * '''Tsuchi-ikazuchi:''' (Earth thunder) * '''Waka-ikazuchi:''' (Young thunder)

revision.text:
'''Yakusanoikazuchi''' or '''ikazuchi''' is a collective name for the eight [[kami]] of [[thunder]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. Each one represents a different type of storm.<ref>{{Cite book|last1=Fr?d?ric|first1=Louis|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&q=Ikazuchi+myth&pg=PA376|title=Japan Encyclopedia|last2=Louis-Frédéric|date=2005|publisher=Harvard University Press|isbn=978-0-674-01753-5|pages=376|language=en}}</ref>
Ikazuchi (雷) literally means thunder in Japanese.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.japandict.com/%E9%9B%B7|title=JapanDict Japanese dictionary - Definition of 雷|website=www.japandict.com}}</ref>
== Mythology == Some tellings say that the eight deities were from the maggots of [[Izanami|Izanami's]] rotting corpse. Many versions of the [[Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki]] state that after [[Izanagi]] saw his wife in such a state and fled in terror, Izanami sent the [[Shikome|Yomotsu-shikome]], 150 warriors, and the eight thunder kami after him.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=175|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yakusanoikazuchi|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|last=Williams|first=George|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=-hmFOZe7m4MC&dq=eight+thunder+kami&pg=PA49|title=Shinto|date=2009|publisher=Infobase Publishing|isbn=978-1-4381-0646-5|pages=49|language=en}}</ref>
== List ==
* '''Fusu-ikazuchi:''' (Couchant thunder)<ref>{{Cite book|last=Herbert|first=Jean|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&dq=ikazuchi+shinto&pg=PT347|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|date=2010-10-18|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=May 2024}} * '''Hono-ikazuchi:''' (Fire thunder) the Kojiki says that this kami came from Izanami's breasts. * '''Kuro-ikazuchi:''' (Black thunder) The Kojiki says that this deity came from Izanami's belly. However, the Nihon Shoki says that Kuro-ikazuchi was in Izanami's [[rectum]]. * '''Naru-ikazuchi:''' (Rumbling thunder) * '''Ō-ikazuchi:''' (Great thunder) both the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] state that Ō-ikazuchi came from Izanami's head. * '''Saku-ikazuchi:''' (Cleaving thunder) * '''Tsuchi-ikazuchi:''' (Earth thunder) * '''Waka-ikazuchi:''' (Young thunder)

Many versions of the [[Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki]] state that after [[Izanagi]] saw his wife in such a state and fled in terror, Izanami sent the [[Shikome|Yomotsu-shikome]], 150 warriors, and the eight thunder kami after him.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=175|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yakusanoikazuchi|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|last=Williams|first=George|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=-hmFOZe7m4MC&dq=eight+thunder+kami&pg=PA49|title=Shinto|date=2009|publisher=Infobase Publishing|isbn=978-1-4381-0646-5|pages=49|language=en}}</ref>
== List ==
* '''Fusu-ikazuchi:''' (Couchant thunder)<ref>{{Cite book|last=Herbert|first=Jean|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&dq=ikazuchi+shinto&pg=PT347|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|date=2010-10-18|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=May 2024}} * '''Hono-ikazuchi:''' (Fire thunder) the Kojiki says that this kami came from Izanami's breasts. * '''Kuro-ikazuchi:''' (Black thunder) The Kojiki says that this deity came from Izanami's belly. However, the Nihon Shoki says that Kuro-ikazuchi was in Izanami's [[rectum]]. * '''Naru-ikazuchi:''' (Rumbling thunder) * '''Ō-ikazuchi:''' (Great thunder) both the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] state that Ō-ikazuchi came from Izanami's head. * '''Saku-ikazuchi:''' (Cleaving thunder) * '''Tsuchi-ikazuchi:''' (Earth thunder) * '''Waka-ikazuchi:''' (Young thunder)

'''Yakusanoikazuchi''' or '''ikazuchi''' is a collective name for the eight [[kami]] of [[thunder]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. Each one represents a different type of storm.<ref>{{Cite book|last1=Fr?d?ric|first1=Louis|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&q=Ikazuchi+myth&pg=PA376|title=Japan Encyclopedia|last2=Louis-Frédéric|date=2005|publisher=Harvard University Press|isbn=978-0-674-01753-5|pages=376|language=en}}</ref>
Ikazuchi (雷) literally means thunder in Japanese.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.japandict.com/%E9%9B%B7|title=JapanDict Japanese dictionary - Definition of 雷|website=www.japandict.com}}</ref>
== Mythology == Some tellings say that the eight deities were from the maggots of [[Izanami|Izanami's]] rotting corpse. Many versions of the [[Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki]] state that after [[Izanagi]] saw his wife in such a state and fled in terror, Izanami sent the [[Shikome|Yomotsu-shikome]], 150 warriors, and the eight thunder kami after him.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=175|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yakusanoikazuchi|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|last=Williams|first=George|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=-hmFOZe7m4MC&dq=eight+thunder+kami&pg=PA49|title=Shinto|date=2009|publisher=Infobase Publishing|isbn=978-1-4381-0646-5|pages=49|language=en}}</ref>
== List ==
* '''Fusu-ikazuchi:''' (Couchant thunder)<ref>{{Cite book|last=Herbert|first=Jean|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&dq=ikazuchi+shinto&pg=PT347|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|date=2010-10-18|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=May 2024}} * '''Hono-ikazuchi:''' (Fire thunder) the Kojiki says that this kami came from Izanami's breasts. * '''Kuro-ikazuchi:''' (Black thunder) The Kojiki says that this deity came from Izanami's belly. However, the Nihon Shoki says that Kuro-ikazuchi was in Izanami's [[rectum]]. * '''Naru-ikazuchi:''' (Rumbling thunder) * '''Ō-ikazuchi:''' (Great thunder) both the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] state that Ō-ikazuchi came from Izanami's head. * '''Saku-ikazuchi:''' (Cleaving thunder) * '''Tsuchi-ikazuchi:''' (Earth thunder) * '''Waka-ikazuchi:''' (Young thunder)

Ikazuchi (雷) literally means thunder in Japanese.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.japandict.com/%E9%9B%B7|title=JapanDict Japanese dictionary - Definition of 雷|website=www.japandict.com}}</ref>
== Mythology == Some tellings say that the eight deities were from the maggots of [[Izanami|Izanami's]] rotting corpse. Many versions of the [[Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki]] state that after [[Izanagi]] saw his wife in such a state and fled in terror, Izanami sent the [[Shikome|Yomotsu-shikome]], 150 warriors, and the eight thunder kami after him.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=175|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yakusanoikazuchi|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|last=Williams|first=George|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=-hmFOZe7m4MC&dq=eight+thunder+kami&pg=PA49|title=Shinto|date=2009|publisher=Infobase Publishing|isbn=978-1-4381-0646-5|pages=49|language=en}}</ref>
== List ==
* '''Fusu-ikazuchi:''' (Couchant thunder)<ref>{{Cite book|last=Herbert|first=Jean|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&dq=ikazuchi+shinto&pg=PT347|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|date=2010-10-18|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=May 2024}} * '''Hono-ikazuchi:''' (Fire thunder) the Kojiki says that this kami came from Izanami's breasts. * '''Kuro-ikazuchi:''' (Black thunder) The Kojiki says that this deity came from Izanami's belly. However, the Nihon Shoki says that Kuro-ikazuchi was in Izanami's [[rectum]]. * '''Naru-ikazuchi:''' (Rumbling thunder) * '''Ō-ikazuchi:''' (Great thunder) both the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] state that Ō-ikazuchi came from Izanami's head. * '''Saku-ikazuchi:''' (Cleaving thunder) * '''Tsuchi-ikazuchi:''' (Earth thunder) * '''Waka-ikazuchi:''' (Young thunder)

'''Yakusanoikazuchi''' or '''ikazuchi''' is a collective name for the eight [[kami]] of [[thunder]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. Each one represents a different type of storm.<ref>{{Cite book|last1=Fr?d?ric|first1=Louis|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&q=Ikazuchi+myth&pg=PA376|title=Japan Encyclopedia|last2=Louis-Frédéric|date=2005|publisher=Harvard University Press|isbn=978-0-674-01753-5|pages=376|language=en}}</ref>
Ikazuchi (雷) literally means thunder in Japanese.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.japandict.com/%E9%9B%B7|title=JapanDict Japanese dictionary - Definition of 雷|website=www.japandict.com}}</ref>
== Mythology == Some tellings say that the eight deities were from the maggots of [[Izanami|Izanami's]] rotting corpse. Many versions of the [[Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki]] state that after [[Izanagi]] saw his wife in such a state and fled in terror, Izanami sent the [[Shikome|Yomotsu-shikome]], 150 warriors, and the eight thunder kami after him.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=175|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yakusanoikazuchi|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|last=Williams|first=George|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=-hmFOZe7m4MC&dq=eight+thunder+kami&pg=PA49|title=Shinto|date=2009|publisher=Infobase Publishing|isbn=978-1-4381-0646-5|pages=49|language=en}}</ref>
== List ==

However, the Nihon Shoki says that Kuro-ikazuchi was in Izanami's [[rectum]]. * '''Naru-ikazuchi:''' (Rumbling thunder) * '''Ō-ikazuchi:''' (Great thunder) both the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] state that Ō-ikazuchi came from Izanami's head. * '''Saku-ikazuchi:''' (Cleaving thunder) * '''Tsuchi-ikazuchi:''' (Earth thunder) * '''Waka-ikazuchi:''' (Young thunder)

== Mythology == Some tellings say that the eight deities were from the maggots of [[Izanami|Izanami's]] rotting corpse. Many versions of the [[Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki]] state that after [[Izanagi]] saw his wife in such a state and fled in terror, Izanami sent the [[Shikome|Yomotsu-shikome]], 150 warriors, and the eight thunder kami after him.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=175|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yakusanoikazuchi|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|last=Williams|first=George|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=-hmFOZe7m4MC&dq=eight+thunder+kami&pg=PA49|title=Shinto|date=2009|publisher=Infobase Publishing|isbn=978-1-4381-0646-5|pages=49|language=en}}</ref>
== List ==
* '''Fusu-ikazuchi:''' (Couchant thunder)<ref>{{Cite book|last=Herbert|first=Jean|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&dq=ikazuchi+shinto&pg=PT347|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|date=2010-10-18|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=May 2024}} * '''Hono-ikazuchi:''' (Fire thunder) the Kojiki says that this kami came from Izanami's breasts. * '''Kuro-ikazuchi:''' (Black thunder) The Kojiki says that this deity came from Izanami's belly. However, the Nihon Shoki says that Kuro-ikazuchi was in Izanami's [[rectum]]. * '''Naru-ikazuchi:''' (Rumbling thunder) * '''Ō-ikazuchi:''' (Great thunder) both the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] state that Ō-ikazuchi came from Izanami's head. * '''Saku-ikazuchi:''' (Cleaving thunder) * '''Tsuchi-ikazuchi:''' (Earth thunder) * '''Waka-ikazuchi:''' (Young thunder)

revision.text:
'''Yakusanoikazuchi''' or '''ikazuchi''' is a collective name for the eight [[kami]] of [[thunder]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. Each one represents a different type of storm.<ref>{{Cite book|last1=Fr?d?ric|first1=Louis|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&q=Ikazuchi+myth&pg=PA376|title=Japan Encyclopedia|last2=Louis-Frédéric|date=2005|publisher=Harvard University Press|isbn=978-0-674-01753-5|pages=376|language=en}}</ref>
Ikazuchi (雷) literally means thunder in Japanese.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.japandict.com/%E9%9B%B7|title=JapanDict Japanese dictionary - Definition of 雷|website=www.japandict.com}}</ref>
== Mythology == Some tellings say that the eight deities were from the maggots of [[Izanami|Izanami's]] rotting corpse. Many versions of the [[Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki]] state that after [[Izanagi]] saw his wife in such a state and fled in terror, Izanami sent the [[Shikome|Yomotsu-shikome]], 150 warriors, and the eight thunder kami after him.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=175|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yakusanoikazuchi|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|last=Williams|first=George|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=-hmFOZe7m4MC&dq=eight+thunder+kami&pg=PA49|title=Shinto|date=2009|publisher=Infobase Publishing|isbn=978-1-4381-0646-5|pages=49|language=en}}</ref>
== List ==
* '''Fusu-ikazuchi:''' (Couchant thunder)<ref>{{Cite book|last=Herbert|first=Jean|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&dq=ikazuchi+shinto&pg=PT347|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|date=2010-10-18|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=May 2024}} * '''Hono-ikazuchi:''' (Fire thunder) the Kojiki says that this kami came from Izanami's breasts. * '''Kuro-ikazuchi:''' (Black thunder) The Kojiki says that this deity came from Izanami's belly. However, the Nihon Shoki says that Kuro-ikazuchi was in Izanami's [[rectum]]. * '''Naru-ikazuchi:''' (Rumbling thunder) * '''Ō-ikazuchi:''' (Great thunder) both the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] state that Ō-ikazuchi came from Izanami's head. * '''Saku-ikazuchi:''' (Cleaving thunder) * '''Tsuchi-ikazuchi:''' (Earth thunder) * '''Waka-ikazuchi:''' (Young thunder)
== References == {{reflist}}
‎]]
{{Japan-myth-stub}}

However, the Nihon Shoki says that Kuro-ikazuchi was in Izanami's [[rectum]]. * '''Naru-ikazuchi:''' (Rumbling thunder) * '''Ō-ikazuchi:''' (Great thunder) both the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] state that Ō-ikazuchi came from Izanami's head. * '''Saku-ikazuchi:''' (Cleaving thunder) * '''Tsuchi-ikazuchi:''' (Earth thunder) * '''Waka-ikazuchi:''' (Young thunder)

Many versions of the [[Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki]] state that after [[Izanagi]] saw his wife in such a state and fled in terror, Izanami sent the [[:en:Shikome|Yomotsu-shikome]], 150 warriors, and the eight thunder kami after him.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=175|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yakusanoikazuchi|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|last=Williams|first=George|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=-hmFOZe7m4MC&dq=eight+thunder+kami&pg=PA49|title=Shinto|date=2009|publisher=Infobase Publishing|isbn=978-1-4381-0646-5|pages=49|language=en}}</ref>
== List ==
* '''Fusu-ikazuchi:''' (Couchant thunder)<ref>{{Cite book|last=Herbert|first=Jean|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&dq=ikazuchi+shinto&pg=PT347|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|date=2010-10-18|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=May 2024}} * '''Hono-ikazuchi:''' (Fire thunder) the Kojiki says that this kami came from Izanami's breasts. * '''Kuro-ikazuchi:''' (Black thunder) The Kojiki says that this deity came from Izanami's belly. However, the Nihon Shoki says that Kuro-ikazuchi was in Izanami's [[:en:rectum|rectum]]. * '''Naru-ikazuchi:''' (Rumbling thunder) * '''Ō-ikazuchi:''' (Great thunder) both the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] state that Ō-ikazuchi came from Izanami's head. * '''Saku-ikazuchi:''' (Cleaving thunder) * '''Tsuchi-ikazuchi:''' (Earth thunder) * '''Waka-ikazuchi:''' (Young thunder)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yakusanoikazuchi]][[ja:火雷大神]][[zh:八雷神]] {{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|consort={{ill|Tamakushi-hime|WD=Q11572681}}|offspring={{ill|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|WD=Q11634943}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10892534]] Wikidata name: Yakusanoikazuchi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yakusanoikazuchi''' or '''ikazuchi''' is a collective name for the eight [[kami]] of [[:en:Thunder|Thunder]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. Each one represents a different type of storm.<ref>{{Cite book|last1=Fr?d?ric|first1=Louis|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&q=Ikazuchi+myth&pg=PA376|title=Japan Encyclopedia|last2=Louis-Frédéric|date=2005|publisher=Harvard University Press|isbn=978-0-674-01753-5|pages=376|language=en}}</ref>
Ikazuchi (雷) literally means thunder in Japanese.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.japandict.com/%E9%9B%B7|title=JapanDict Japanese dictionary - Definition of 雷|website=www.japandict.com}}</ref>
== Mythology == Some tellings say that the eight deities were from the maggots of [[:en:Izanami|Izanami's]] rotting corpse. Many versions of the [[Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki]] state that after [[Izanagi]] saw his wife in such a state and fled in terror, Izanami sent the [[:en:Shikome|Yomotsu-shikome]], 150 warriors, and the eight thunder kami after him.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=175|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yakusanoikazuchi|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|last=Williams|first=George|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=-hmFOZe7m4MC&dq=eight+thunder+kami&pg=PA49|title=Shinto|date=2009|publisher=Infobase Publishing|isbn=978-1-4381-0646-5|pages=49|language=en}}</ref>
== List ==
* '''Fusu-ikazuchi:''' (Couchant thunder)<ref>{{Cite book|last=Herbert|first=Jean|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&dq=ikazuchi+shinto&pg=PT347|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|date=2010-10-18|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=May 2024}} * '''Hono-ikazuchi:''' (Fire thunder) the Kojiki says that this kami came from Izanami's breasts. * '''Kuro-ikazuchi:''' (Black thunder) The Kojiki says that this deity came from Izanami's belly. However, the Nihon Shoki says that Kuro-ikazuchi was in Izanami's [[:en:rectum|rectum]]. * '''Naru-ikazuchi:''' (Rumbling thunder) * '''Ō-ikazuchi:''' (Great thunder) both the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] state that Ō-ikazuchi came from Izanami's head. * '''Saku-ikazuchi:''' (Cleaving thunder) * '''Tsuchi-ikazuchi:''' (Earth thunder) * '''Waka-ikazuchi:''' (Young thunder)
== References == {{reflist}}
‎]]

}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yakusanoikazuchi]][[ja:火雷大神]][[zh:八雷神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yakusanoikazuchi]][[ja:火雷大神]][[zh:八雷神]] {{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|consort={{ill|Tamakushi-hime|WD=Q11572681}}|offspring={{ill|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|WD=Q11634943}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10892534]] Wikidata name: Yakusanoikazuchi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Yakusanoikazuchi''' or '''ikazuchi''' is a collective name for the eight [[kami]] of [[:en:Thunder|Thunder]] in [[Japanese mythology]]. Each one represents a different type of storm.<ref>{{Cite book|last1=Fr?d?ric|first1=Louis|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&q=Ikazuchi+myth&pg=PA376|title=Japan Encyclopedia|last2=Louis-Frédéric|date=2005|publisher=Harvard University Press|isbn=978-0-674-01753-5|pages=376|language=en}}</ref>
Ikazuchi (雷) literally means thunder in Japanese.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.japandict.com/%E9%9B%B7|title=JapanDict Japanese dictionary - Definition of 雷|website=www.japandict.com}}</ref>
== Mythology == Some tellings say that the eight deities were from the maggots of [[:en:Izanami|Izanami's]] rotting corpse. Many versions of the [[Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki]] state that after [[Izanagi]] saw his wife in such a state and fled in terror, Izanami sent the [[:en:Shikome|Yomotsu-shikome]], 150 warriors, and the eight thunder kami after him.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=175|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yakusanoikazuchi|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|last=Williams|first=George|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=-hmFOZe7m4MC&dq=eight+thunder+kami&pg=PA49|title=Shinto|date=2009|publisher=Infobase Publishing|isbn=978-1-4381-0646-5|pages=49|language=en}}</ref>
== List ==
* '''Fusu-ikazuchi:''' (Couchant thunder)<ref>{{Cite book|last=Herbert|first=Jean|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&dq=ikazuchi+shinto&pg=PT347|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|date=2010-10-18|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=May 2024}} * '''Hono-ikazuchi:''' (Fire thunder) the Kojiki says that this kami came from Izanami's breasts. * '''Kuro-ikazuchi:''' (Black thunder) The Kojiki says that this deity came from Izanami's belly. However, the Nihon Shoki says that Kuro-ikazuchi was in Izanami's [[:en:rectum|rectum]]. * '''Naru-ikazuchi:''' (Rumbling thunder) * '''Ō-ikazuchi:''' (Great thunder) both the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] state that Ō-ikazuchi came from Izanami's head. * '''Saku-ikazuchi:''' (Cleaving thunder) * '''Tsuchi-ikazuchi:''' (Earth thunder) * '''Waka-ikazuchi:''' (Young thunder)
== References == {{reflist}}
‎]]

Ikazuchi (雷) literally means thunder in Japanese.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.japandict.com/%E9%9B%B7|title=JapanDict Japanese dictionary - Definition of 雷|website=www.japandict.com}}</ref>
== Mythology == Some tellings say that the eight deities were from the maggots of [[:en:Izanami|Izanami's]] rotting corpse. Many versions of the [[Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki]] state that after [[Izanagi]] saw his wife in such a state and fled in terror, Izanami sent the [[:en:Shikome|Yomotsu-shikome]], 150 warriors, and the eight thunder kami after him.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=175|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yakusanoikazuchi|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|last=Williams|first=George|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=-hmFOZe7m4MC&dq=eight+thunder+kami&pg=PA49|title=Shinto|date=2009|publisher=Infobase Publishing|isbn=978-1-4381-0646-5|pages=49|language=en}}</ref>
== List ==
* '''Fusu-ikazuchi:''' (Couchant thunder)<ref>{{Cite book|last=Herbert|first=Jean|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&dq=ikazuchi+shinto&pg=PT347|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|date=2010-10-18|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=May 2024}} * '''Hono-ikazuchi:''' (Fire thunder) the Kojiki says that this kami came from Izanami's breasts. * '''Kuro-ikazuchi:''' (Black thunder) The Kojiki says that this deity came from Izanami's belly. However, the Nihon Shoki says that Kuro-ikazuchi was in Izanami's [[:en:rectum|rectum]]. * '''Naru-ikazuchi:''' (Rumbling thunder) * '''Ō-ikazuchi:''' (Great thunder) both the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] state that Ō-ikazuchi came from Izanami's head. * '''Saku-ikazuchi:''' (Cleaving thunder) * '''Tsuchi-ikazuchi:''' (Earth thunder) * '''Waka-ikazuchi:''' (Young thunder)
== References == {{reflist}}
‎]]

Each one represents a different type of storm.<ref>{{Cite book|last1=Fr?d?ric|first1=Louis|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&q=Ikazuchi+myth&pg=PA376|title=Japan Encyclopedia|last2=Louis-Frédéric|date=2005|publisher=Harvard University Press|isbn=978-0-674-01753-5|pages=376|language=en}}</ref>
Ikazuchi (雷) literally means thunder in Japanese.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.japandict.com/%E9%9B%B7|title=JapanDict Japanese dictionary - Definition of 雷|website=www.japandict.com}}</ref>
== Mythology == Some tellings say that the eight deities were from the maggots of {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami's}} rotting corpse. Many versions of the [[Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki]] state that after [[Izanagi]] saw his wife in such a state and fled in terror, Izanami sent the {{ill|Shikome|en|Shikome|WD=Q2390449|lt=Yomotsu-shikome}}, 150 warriors, and the eight thunder kami after him.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=175|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yakusanoikazuchi|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|last=Williams|first=George|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=-hmFOZe7m4MC&dq=eight+thunder+kami&pg=PA49|title=Shinto|date=2009|publisher=Infobase Publishing|isbn=978-1-4381-0646-5|pages=49|language=en}}</ref>
== List ==
* '''Fusu-ikazuchi:''' (Couchant thunder)<ref>{{Cite book|last=Herbert|first=Jean|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&dq=ikazuchi+shinto&pg=PT347|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|date=2010-10-18|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=May 2024}} * '''Hono-ikazuchi:''' (Fire thunder) the Kojiki says that this kami came from Izanami's breasts. * '''Kuro-ikazuchi:''' (Black thunder) The Kojiki says that this deity came from Izanami's belly. However, the Nihon Shoki says that Kuro-ikazuchi was in Izanami's {{ill|rectum|en|rectum|WD=Q158716|lt=rectum}}. * '''Naru-ikazuchi:''' (Rumbling thunder) * '''Ō-ikazuchi:''' (Great thunder) both the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] state that Ō-ikazuchi came from Izanami's head. * '''Saku-ikazuchi:''' (Cleaving thunder) * '''Tsuchi-ikazuchi:''' (Earth thunder) * '''Waka-ikazuchi:''' (Young thunder)
== References == {{reflist}}
‎]]
{{Japan-myth-stub}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yakusanoikazuchi]][[ja:火雷大神]][[zh:八雷神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yakusanoikazuchi]][[ja:火雷大神]][[zh:八雷神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Yakusanoikazuchi]][[ja:火雷大神]][[zh:八雷神]] {{Infobox deity|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|consort={{ill|Tamakushi-hime|WD=Q11572681|1=Tamakushi-hime}}|offspring={{ill|Kamo Wake-ikazuchi|WD=Q11634943|1=Kamo-wake-ikazuchi-no-Kami}}}} '''Yakusanoikazuchi''' or '''ikazuchi''' is a collective name for the eight [[kami]] of {{ill|Thunder|en|Thunder|WD=Q83301|lt=Thunder|1=Thunder}} in [[Japanese mythology]]. Each one represents a different type of storm.<ref>{{Cite book|last1=Fr?d?ric|first1=Louis|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&q=Ikazuchi+myth&pg=PA376|title=Japan Encyclopedia|last2=Louis-Frédéric|date=2005|publisher=Harvard University Press|isbn=978-0-674-01753-5|pages=376|language=en}}</ref>
Ikazuchi (雷) literally means thunder in Japanese.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.japandict.com/%E9%9B%B7|title=JapanDict Japanese dictionary - Definition of 雷|website=www.japandict.com}}</ref>
== Mythology == Some tellings say that the eight deities were from the maggots of {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami's|1=Izanami}} rotting corpse. Many versions of the [[Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki]] state that after [[Izanagi]] saw his wife in such a state and fled in terror, Izanami sent the {{ill|Shikome|en|Shikome|WD=Q2390449|lt=Yomotsu-shikome|1=Shikome}}, 150 warriors, and the eight thunder kami after him.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp/modules/xwords/entry.php?entryID=175|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto - Home : Kami in Classic Texts : Yakusanoikazuchi|website=eos.kokugakuin.ac.jp}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|last=Williams|first=George|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=-hmFOZe7m4MC&dq=eight+thunder+kami&pg=PA49|title=Shinto|date=2009|publisher=Infobase Publishing|isbn=978-1-4381-0646-5|pages=49|language=en}}</ref>
== List ==
* '''Fusu-ikazuchi:''' (Couchant thunder)<ref>{{Cite book|last=Herbert|first=Jean|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=Sg_JBQAAQBAJ&dq=ikazuchi+shinto&pg=PT347|title=Shinto: At the Fountainhead of Japan|date=2010-10-18|publisher=Routledge|isbn=978-1-136-90376-2|language=en}}</ref>{{Page needed|date=May 2024}} * '''Hono-ikazuchi:''' (Fire thunder) the Kojiki says that this kami came from Izanami's breasts. * '''Kuro-ikazuchi:''' (Black thunder) The Kojiki says that this deity came from Izanami's belly. However, the Nihon Shoki says that Kuro-ikazuchi was in Izanami's {{ill|rectum|en|rectum|WD=Q158716|lt=rectum|1=Rectum}}. * '''Naru-ikazuchi:''' (Rumbling thunder) * '''Ō-ikazuchi:''' (Great thunder) both the [[Kojiki]] and the [[Nihon Shoki]] state that Ō-ikazuchi came from Izanami's head. * '''Saku-ikazuchi:''' (Cleaving thunder) * '''Tsuchi-ikazuchi:''' (Earth thunder) * '''Waka-ikazuchi:''' (Young thunder)
== References == {{reflist}}
‎]]
{{Japan-myth-stub}}
